# Burn-scar inference vs. ICNF Áreas Ardidas — overlap crosscheck

<!-- generated by: scripts/09_burn_scar_audit.py at b38c6dd0fbaf18e0a6f478c3cedb6b7a0a2746b5 -->

COG: `outputs/cogs/burn_scar_20260610T072820Z.tif` (run `20260610T072820Z`, model `ibm-nasa-geospatial/Prithvi-EO-2.0-300M-BurnScars` @ `a3f2c410`).
Overlap window: **2025-06-09 .. 2025-12-31** (COG trailing window ∩ ICNF vintage, MapServer layer 20).

| metric | value |
|---|---|
| valid pixels | 8713084 |
| ICNF polygons in overlap window | 16 |
| burned pixels (rasterized) | 4341 |
| burned base rate | 0.00050 |
| Spearman ρ (probability vs. burned mask) | 0.0164 |
| top-decile lift (burned rate in top 10% ÷ base rate) | 3.25× |

Both rasters describe burn scars of fires that already happened; the
correlation measures how well the model's *relative* scores rank known
burned area within this AOI and window. It is not a calibration claim
and not a forecast skill claim.

## Domain-shift sanity check (close-out caveat #2)

Inference agreement against the three largest ICNF-2025 perimeters in the
wider region, truth = union of all in-window mapped burns per inference box:

| OBJECTID | concelho | area (ha) | fire | S2 window | truth polys | scenes | IoU@0.5 | precision | recall | mean prob in/out |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 112769 | Arganil | 65853 | 2025-08-13..2025-08-25 | 2025-06-29..2025-10-09 | 13 | 51 | 0.746 | 0.747 | 0.999 | 0.993 / 0.299 |
| 111188 | Arouca | 4519 | 2025-07-28..2025-07-28 | 2025-06-13..2025-09-11 | 6 | 38 | 0.721 | 0.726 | 0.992 | 0.969 / 0.287 |
| 111504 | Castro Daire | 2074 | 2025-09-19..2025-09-20 | 2025-08-05..2025-11-04 | 3 | 31 | 0.671 | 0.682 | 0.977 | 0.958 / 0.205 |

## Interpretation (read before using `recent_burn_share_12mo`)

1. **The model transfers.** On real fires with substantial burned area the
   US-HLS-trained checkpoint recovers 98–100 % of ICNF-mapped burn at
   IoU 0.67–0.75, with a wide score separation inside vs. outside
   perimeters (≈0.96–0.99 vs. ≈0.21–0.30). This is the load-bearing
   evidence for the feature.
2. **The pilot-AOI Spearman is uninformative, not alarming.** Only ~43 ha
   of ICNF-mapped burn fell inside the pilot AOI during the overlap window
   (burned base rate 0.0005). At that prevalence a pixel-level rank
   correlation is dominated by ties and noise; the top-decile lift (3.25×)
   is the meaningful readout and shows positive rank association. Re-run
   after the 2026 vintage publishes, or on an AOI with a major in-window
   fire, before quoting a headline correlation.
3. **Max-composite inflation caveat.** The pilot raster's median score is
   ≈0.47 because a 179-scene max-composite keeps each pixel's highest
   single-scene score: any one-scene false positive sticks. Stage 2 must
   not read the composite as areal burn share at face value with the 0.5
   threshold; treat thresholded shares as *relative* rank inputs (which is
   all `config/exposure_score.yaml` requires) and revisit the threshold in
   WU-6 if ranked shares saturate.

All values above are relative model scores for *burn scars* — post-event
spectral signatures of fires that already happened. Nothing here is a
calibrated probability, an ignition prediction, or a forecast.
